name: network-diagram-mcp
description: Network topology editor — Claude builds diagrams via MCP. nmap/CSV import, one-click SSH.
long_description:
Visual network topology editor with 44 MCP tools. Claude / Cursor / Claude Desktop edit a live in-browser diagram at https://map.den.dance/ via a stdio↔WebSocket bridge. Features: CRUD on nodes / connections / stickies, multi-sheet workspaces, smart auto-layout (force-directed, cluster-by-type, cluster-by-connection), one-click SSH and web-service access from any node card, `nmap -oX` and CSV inventory import with auto-typing, JSON / PNG / PDF export. Local-first, no cloud. Open-source successor to netViz (CA Technologies, 1990–2012).

Requires an open NetMap browser tab with Settings → ⚙ → MCP Agent Bridge → Enable WebSocket connection — the bundle runs the stdio MCP server locally and bridges to the tab over WebSocket on localhost.

netmap_port.title: WebSocket Port
netmap_port.description: Local WebSocket port the bridge listens on. Must match the URL configured in the NetMap browser tab's Settings → MCP Agent Bridge.
